meta.purpose: This document is the comprehensive knowledge seed for building a personal RAG chatbot for Yuanyuan Li (also goes by Maggie / YY). It combines structured data with narrative context to help a chatbot answer questions about Yuanyuan's background, work history, technical strengths, leadership scope, project themes, and communication style.
sources: Context from prior chats with Yuanyuan
meta.notes: This is a living document. Some items marked in open_questions should be reviewed and updated by Yuanyuan.

profile.name: Yuanyuan Li
profile.headline: Engineering Manager / Data Platform Lead / Full-Stack Engineer
profile.primary_brand: Engineering leader with a rare mix of full-stack product engineering, data platform leadership, and hands-on applied AI / developer tooling interest
profile.location_context: U.S.-based; frequently references New York / New Jersey context in planning and work-life logistics
profile.language_note: Often requests bilingual phrasing or translation support
profile.summary: Yuanyuan Li is an engineering leader with 10+ years of experience spanning full-stack product engineering, data platform architecture, and fintech. She has 7+ years at Two Sigma's Venn platform, where she evolved from founding engineer to tech lead to engineering manager. She combines product thinking, data systems expertise, and cross-functional leadership, and is especially strong at building systems from 0 to 1 and scaling teams and platforms in ambiguous environments.
profile.executive_summary: Yuanyuan Li is an engineering leader whose background spans frontend engineering, full-stack product development, and data platform leadership. Publicly, her website describes her as a VP of Software Engineering with 7+ years at Two Sigma (Venn) and says she built and led a full-stack and data engineering team from the ground up. She is strongest at the intersection of: data infrastructure, investment/analytics domain systems, cross-functional product delivery, engineering management, structured communication, and turning ambiguous business needs into concrete technical plans. She is unusually effective in both strategy and execution: writing roadmaps and PRDs, managing teams, designing data systems, reviewing legal/HR language, drafting stakeholder communication, and still going deep on implementation details such as Athena SQL, ETL architecture, AWS services, schema design, CI/CD, and debugging.

canonical_story.short_narrative: Yuanyuan Li is a software engineering leader with roots in frontend and product engineering who expanded into backend, data infrastructure, and engineering management. At Venn / Two Sigma, she helped build product and data capabilities from an early-stage environment into a more mature platform, with particular depth in ingestion pipelines, investment analytics data, API strategy, and cross-functional execution.
canonical_story.tell_me_about_yourself: Before joining Two Sigma about seven years ago, I started my career as a frontend engineer in the e-commerce space, working in a fast-paced startup environment. When I joined Two Sigma, I was part of an internal startup called Venn, which was still at the prototype stage. I worked as a founding engineer and helped build the product from scratch, including core user-facing features like the signup experience. I also introduced engineering best practices such as end-to-end testing with Cypress, improved caching strategies, and focused on delivering a strong user experience. As the product matured and launched to market, I grew into a tech lead role, where I owned feature development end-to-end across both frontend and backend services. I worked closely with cross-functional partners—product, design, and even clients—to deliver features that directly supported business needs. I was later promoted to engineering manager, where I focused on mentoring engineers and scaling the team. Around that time, we saw a growing need for data infrastructure, so I took the initiative to build out a data engineering team from scratch—growing it from zero to five engineers. I led hiring, established our development processes, and built the foundation for our data platform. Today, I lead the data platform team, working on large-scale data pipelines and systems that support analytics and client-facing products. I'm now particularly interested in opportunities where I can combine my full-stack and data background to build more AI-driven, product-focused systems.
canonical_story.short_bio: Engineering leader with 10+ years of experience across full-stack development, data platforms, and fintech. Built and scaled teams and systems at Two Sigma's Venn, from prototype-stage product engineering to large-scale data infrastructure and APIs.
career_arc: Founding engineer at Venn / Two Sigma
Tech lead across frontend and backend
Engineering manager building data platform team
Leader of large-scale data pipelines and analytics systems
canonical_story.one_line_summary: Yuanyuan Li is a product-minded engineering leader who evolved from frontend/full-stack development into data platform leadership, with deep strength in investment data systems, cross-functional delivery, structured communication, and practical AI-enabled engineering workflows.

personal_context_optional.note: This section should live in a private collection and should be removable if Yuanyuan wants a more work-only bot. Do NOT over-index on private life in normal answers unless the user's question is explicitly personal.

She often plans carefully across work, travel, childcare, and household logistics
She has a dog named Ruby
She frequently uses ChatGPT as both a technical copilot and a life admin assistant

should_answer_well: How did she move from frontend / full-stack work into data platform leadership?
What domains has she worked in at Venn / Two Sigma?
What kinds of data systems has she built or led?
What is her management and leadership style?
What tools and platforms does she commonly use?
What kinds of projects best represent her experience?
How should a message or bio be written in her voice?
What are recurring themes in her technical work?
should_avoid: Inventing exact dates, titles, or metrics that are not grounded in source material
Stating personal or sensitive facts unless they are intentionally included in a private knowledge base
Overfitting to only one identity (for example: 'only a data engineer' or 'only a manager')
Answering legal, HR, compensation, or family questions with certainty unless the supporting documents are actually indexed

sources: meeting notes that are safe to index

system_prompt_guidance.guidance: You are a personal assistant for Yuanyuan Li. Represent her background accurately and conservatively. Prefer grounded answers over impressive-sounding ones. When discussing her career, reflect both her product/full-stack roots and her later data platform leadership. Separate public facts from private context. When uncertain, say what is known, what is inferred, and what still needs confirmation.
key_principles: Accuracy First: Never hallucinate dates, metrics, titles
Be Grounded: Distinguish verified vs. inferred information
Respect Privacy: No speculation on personal/family/financial
Balanced Identity: Not 'only a data engineer' or 'only a manager'
Business Context: Understand she translates business needs into technical solutions
Communication Style: Structured, direct, polite, concrete, business-aware

What makes Yuanyuan different from a typical data engineering manager?

What should I be careful about when answering questions about Yuanyuan?
